summary: Exercise therapy is recommended for chronic LBP. McKenzie method for acute disc-related pain. Avoid bed rest.
Multimodal approach: exercise + manual therapy. Deep neck flexor training for chronic neck pain. Graded activity for WAD.
Exercise therapy is first-line. Subacromial decompression NOT superior to exercise. Progressive loading approach.
Combined hip and knee strengthening is superior to knee alone. Patellar taping as adjunct. Address biomechanics.
Eccentric loading (Alfredson) or HSR programme. Load management crucial. Mid-portion vs insertional requires different approach.
Eccentric exercise and isometric loading effective. Tyler Twist (FlexBar) evidence-based. Avoid passive treatments alone.
Structured 9-12 month rehabilitation. Criteria-based return to sport. Quad strength >90% LSI. Address psychological readiness.
Early mobilisation superior to immobilisation. Balance training prevents recurrence. Address peroneal strength.
Exercise therapy is first-line. Land-based and aquatic exercise both effective. Weight management important. Delay arthroplasty with exercise.
key_references: Svege et al. Ann Rheum Dis 2015

summary: Night splinting and nerve/tendon gliding exercises for mild-moderate. Surgical release for severe or failed conservative.
High-load strength training (Rathleff protocol) is effective. Address calf tightness. Orthotics as adjunct.
Cervical mobilisation C1-2 and exercise therapy are effective. SNAG technique has strong evidence. Address deep neck flexors.
Thoracic mobilisation and exercise therapy are effective. Address postural factors. Erector spinae strengthening important.
Stay active, avoid bed rest. Reassurance and education are first-line. Manual therapy and exercise as adjuncts.

key_interventions.intervention: Avoid Imaging in absence of red flags

summary: Exercise therapy is first-line. Combine with cognitive functional therapy or pain neuroscience education.
Conservative care with directional preference exercises and neural mobilisation. Surgical consult if progressive deficit.
Manual therapy + exercise + neural mobilisation. Cervical traction may help selected patients.
Exercise (especially specific neck strengthening) + manual therapy. Avoid prolonged collar use.
Exercise therapy is first-line. Surgery (subacromial decompression) not superior to placebo (Beard 2018 CSAW).
Combination of intra-articular corticosteroid + supervised exercise in early/painful phase. Capsular stretching in stiffness phase.
Exercise + weight loss + education. Avoid arthroscopy for OA (Thorlund 2015).
Loading-based therapy: eccentric (Alfredson) or HSR (Beyer 2015).
Stretching + manual therapy + taping/orthoses + load management. Education on natural history.
POLICE + early mobilisation + exercise therapy. Avoid prolonged immobilisation.
Balance/proprioceptive training is mainstay. Strengthening of peronei.
Eccentric strengthening (Nordic hamstring) is key for prevention & rehab. Criteria-based RTP.
Adductor strengthening (Holmich protocol) is gold standard for athletic groin pain.
Active exercise + education + reassurance. Avoid prolonged collar.
Splinting + neural mobilisation + corticosteroid injection. Surgery if motor deficit or failed conservative care.

condition: Chronic Low Back Pain – Pre-Surgical Optimisation
summary: Exercise therapy is first-line. Combine with cognitive functional therapy or pain neuroscience education. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Conservative care with directional preference exercises and neural mobilisation. Surgical consult if progressive deficit. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Manual therapy + exercise + neural mobilisation. Cervical traction may help selected patients. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Exercise (especially specific neck strengthening) + manual therapy. Avoid prolonged collar use. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Exercise therapy is first-line. Surgery (subacromial decompression) not superior to placebo (Beard 2018 CSAW). (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Combination of intra-articular corticosteroid + supervised exercise in early/painful phase. Capsular stretching in stiffness phase. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Wait-and-see/exercise outperforms corticosteroid injection long-term (Coombes 2013). (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Exercise + education + weight management are first-line. Manual therapy as adjunct. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Exercise + weight loss + education. Avoid arthroscopy for OA (Thorlund 2015). (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Combined hip + knee strengthening, gait retraining, patient education. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Criteria-based progression. Quad strength ≥90% LSI before RTS. Neuromuscular training reduces re-injury. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Loading-based therapy: eccentric (Alfredson) or HSR (Beyer 2015). (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Stretching + manual therapy + taping/orthoses + load management. Education on natural history. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
POLICE + early mobilisation + exercise therapy. Avoid prolonged immobilisation. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Balance/proprioceptive training is mainstay. Strengthening of peronei. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Eccentric strengthening (Nordic hamstring) is key for prevention & rehab. Criteria-based RTP. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Adductor strengthening (Holmich protocol) is gold standard for athletic groin pain. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Active exercise + education + reassurance. Avoid prolonged collar. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
Splinting + neural mobilisation + corticosteroid injection. Surgery if motor deficit or failed conservative care. (pre-surgical optimisation — prehab to maximise post-op outcomes)
